description_long: This is a hand axe featuring a robust, dark metal blade with a pronounced beard and a single sharp cutting edge. The blade is securely mounted onto a wooden handle that is slightly curved for ergonomic grip, with a darker, textured wrap around the midsection for enhanced handling. The handle appears to be made from a dense hardwood, showing a natural brown coloration with visible grain and a reinforced end cap, possibly for durability or hanging. The blade has a matte, weathered finish, suggesting frequent use or intentional aging for aesthetic purposes. The axe is compact, suitable for one-handed use, and would be practical for tasks such as chopping wood or as a utility tool in urban or outdoor settings. No visible branding or decorative engravings are present, giving it a utilitarian appearance.
description: A compact hand axe with a dark, bearded metal blade and a curved wooden handle wrapped for grip. The design is utilitarian and robust, suitable for practical use.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full profile of the axe, highlighting the curved wooden handle and the broad, bearded blade.
description_view_1: Top-down view displaying the narrow width of the blade and handle, with the grip wrap and end cap visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view revealing the other face of the blade and handle, mirroring the features seen in view 0.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the blade's edge and the oval cross-section of the handle, showing the attachment point.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with a dark leather grip wrap, oval cross-section, ergonomic and robust.
attribute_blade_design: Single-edged, bearded steel blade with a broad cutting edge and a pronounced poll, matte finish.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with visible fit between blade and handle.
